name: Laptops
summary: Personal computing laptops, $500-$4000 range, spanning Windows, macOS, and Linux-compatible hardware.

evidence.claim: Manufacturer-stated battery life typically assumes 150-nit brightness and light tasks; real-world coding or browsing use is 60-75% of stated figure.

MIL-STD-810H is a Department of Defense Test Method Standard. The standard explicitly states it 'does not impose design or test specifications. Rather, it describes the environmental tailoring process that results in realistic materiel designs and test methods based on materiel system performance requirements.'
iFixit teardowns of modern ultraportables (MacBook Air M2, XPS 13 9320, ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11) all show soldered LPDDR memory. iFixit Repairability scores reflect this.
Federal Trade Commission Right-to-Repair rulemaking and the 2021 'Nixing the Fix' report document manufacturer practices that obscure repairability and limit aftermarket parts.

criteria.name: cpu_performance
criteria.notes: Normalize across Geekbench 6 or Cinebench R23 scores; separate single-core and multi-core. For coding/browsing, single-core matters more than marketing claims.

criteria.name: ram_gb
criteria.notes: Soldered RAM cannot be upgraded later (E3). Note whether RAM is socketed or LPDDR.

criteria.name: battery_life_hours
criteria.notes: Expect 60-75% of manufacturer claim in real use (E1).

confabulationPatterns.reality: Base configurations of many ThinkPad E-series, HP Pavilion, and Dell Inspiron ship with 8GB as default; 16GB is an upgrade SKU. Verify the specific SKU.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: For the specific SKU/model cited, is the base configuration RAM 8GB, 16GB, or other?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Manufacturer 'all-day' claims assume 150-nit light-task benchmarks (E1). Real-world coding workload is ~60-75% of claim. Any laptop with stated 10+ hours delivers 7+ in practice; 8-hour stated claims deliver ~5.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the cited battery claim account for real-world workload, or is it the manufacturer's best-case figure?

confabulationPatterns.reality: MIL-STD-810 is a manufacturer self-test against selected procedures, not third-party certification (E2).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the MIL-STD-810 claim framed as a third-party certification? If so, flag.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Modern ultraportables commonly solder RAM to the mainboard; storage may be M.2 but RAM is often fixed (E3).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: For the cited model, is the RAM soldered or socketed? Is storage M.2 user-replaceable?

counterfeitSignals.signal: New laptop listed 30%+ below MSRP from a seller without business verification

compatibilityQuestions: What OS do you need — Windows, macOS, or Linux? Linux compatibility varies sharply across models.
Do you have external USB-C / Thunderbolt peripherals that need specific port versions (USB-C 3.2, TB4, etc.)?

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Software subscriptions that replaced perpetual licenses (Office 365, Adobe CC)

repairability.notes: Framework, ThinkPad, older MacBook Pro score high. Modern MacBook Air, M-series soldered configs score low. Dell XPS, HP Spectre variable by generation.

representativeSkus.name: Framework Laptop 13 (AMD, DIY)
representativeSkus.notes: Highest iFixit repairability score among consumer laptops.
